utility_details.utility_summary: The wi_skills run is substantially more effective than the wo_skills run. The skill-equipped agent applied a structured 8-block analytical protocol that covered occupancy risk tier classification (J3), the 'e ônus' debt-transfer clause with legal interpretation (J2), Art. 889 CPC intimation gap for the missing cônjuge (J4), São Paulo-specific ITBI at 3% (J5), a conditional recommendation with specific prerequisite diligences (J7), and a scored risk matrix. Both passed J6 on deságio analysis. Final score: wi_skills 5/7, wo_skills 1/7 — the skill provides significant analytical depth and accuracy for this specialized domain task.

utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills produces a higher quality report. Both agents generated comprehensive reports covering most analytical areas, and both passed all 5 verifier checks. However, the critical differentiator is J4 (missing cônjuge intimation as annulment risk): the wi_skills agent explicitly flagged this procedural compliance gap with Art. 889 CPC reference — one of the key embedded traps — while the wo_skills agent missed it entirely. Consequently, J7 (actionable verdict with conditions) also scores lower for wo_skills because its conditional recommendation omits the cônjuge verification diligence. The wi_skills agent, guided by the 8-block protocol from the leiloeiro-edital skill, produced a more complete and defensible risk analysis (scoring 7/7 on judge items vs 5/7 for wo_skills), which is precisely the difference that matters for an investment committee making a go/no-go decision on a judicial auction.

utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills produces a higher quality report. utility_details.utility_summary: Both agents produced substantive legal memoranda passing all 5 verifier checks. On the judge evaluation items, wi_skills scores 7/7 while wo_skills scores 6/7, with wo_skills failing J4 (parcelamento effective cost computation). The wi_skills agent explicitly invoked the leiloeiro-edital skill protocol (8-block analysis + Armadilhas Comuns checklist) and produced a structured memorandum with a quantified risk score (9/14), a detailed area divergence breakdown, and an explicit parcelamento cost analysis identifying IGP-M + 1%/month as a high effective rate for a 30-day deferral. The wo_skills agent produced a longer document (22767 vs 19315 bytes) and independently identified most key issues through strong baseline legal reasoning — catching the CEF intimation defect, publication violation, garagem exclusion, área não averbada, and embargos risk — but missed the parcelamento indexation cost analysis entirely. The wi_skills output is more comprehensive and methodologically rigorous due to the structured skill protocol, particularly in the parcelamento cost dimension which is a subtle financial trap that the baseline agent overlooked.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced comprehensive reports that passed all 5 verifier checks. On the 7 judge evaluation items: both scored 1 on J1 (laudêmio identification), J2 (debt-based minimum mechanics), J4 (CEF financing/FGTS), J5 (missing matrícula), J6 (comparative ranking), and J7 (occupancy distinction). The wi_skills report (19,402 bytes) is also more detailed and structurally richer than the wo_skills report (17,010 bytes). Conclusion: wi_skills is more effective, with superior framework-specific analysis particularly on the purga da mora timing assessment.

utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is substantially more effective than wo_skills. The wi_skills agent, guided by the leiloeiro-edital SKILL.md protocol (even though the executable invocation failed and it was applied manually), produced a memorandum that correctly identified six out of seven evaluation items: the Art. 887 publication timeline violation (J1), the garagem matrícula exclusion (J2), the área não averbada regularization burden (J3), the parcelamento IGP-M+1%/month cost analysis (J4), the pending CEF Art. 889 intimation defect (J5), the ongoing embargos risk beyond 'sem efeito suspensivo' (J6), and a personalized cumulative risk recommendation (J7). The skill framework enabled a far more comprehensive and domain-specific audit, directly addressing the client's annulment concern with the depth required.

utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills produces a higher quality report than wo_skills. Both reports correctly identify stale valuation (J1), debt transfer via 'e ônus' clause (J2), quantify ITBI at São Paulo's 3% rate (J5), compute deságio on the 2nd auction (J6), and provide actionable conditional recommendations (J7). However, wi_skills uniquely succeeds on two critical items: J3 (occupancy risk tier classification with named tier and cost/timeline estimates) and J4 (missing cônjuge intimation flagged as annulment risk under Art. 889 CPC). These two items represent the most sophisticated legal and operational risk analysis — precisely the 'hidden traps' the test was designed to detect. The skill-equipped agent's structured 8-block protocol ensured systematic coverage of procedural compliance and occupancy risk quantification, which the baseline agent missed entirely. Final score: wi_skills 7/7, wo_skills 5/7.

utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is clearly more effective. The skill-equipped agent produced a more comprehensive 23046-byte memorandum by following the structured 8-block protocol and Armadilhas Comuns checklist, achieving all 7 evaluation items including the difficult garagem matrícula exclusion (J2) and the nuanced embargos de terceiro ongoing risk assessment beyond 'sem efeito suspensivo' (J6). The wo_skills agent (21780 bytes) performed surprisingly well on the more obvious issues — it correctly identified the publication violation, CEF pending intimation, área não averbada, and gave a personalized cumulative recommendation — but missed the garagem exclusion subclause entirely and treated the embargos as largely resolved. The skill's structured protocol provided systematic coverage that prevented the agent from overlooking the hidden garagem matrícula split, which is precisely the type of 'trap' clause the scenario was designed to test. wi_skills: 7/7 items passed; wo_skills: 5/7 items passed.

utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either run. The wo_skills run failed verifier test VC4 (total acquisition cost not found), consistent with its incomplete cost stack that excludes ITBI quantification.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is significantly more effective. While both runs produced readable, well-structured reports with valid recommendations and recognized key surface-level risks (occupied property, 'e ônus' clause, no-go on 1st auction), the wi_skills run demonstrated clear superiority on four critical analytical dimensions: (1) it explicitly flagged the 26-month valuation staleness as a defasagem risk with legal implications; (2) it classified occupancy into a specific risk tier with cost and timeline estimates; (3) it identified the missing cônjuge intimation as an Art. 889 CPC annulment risk — entirely absent in wo_skills; and (4) it produced a complete, itemized cost stack including São Paulo's 3% ITBI rate, which wo_skills explicitly omitted. The wo_skills report, while competent on surface facts, missed two of the most actionable risk flags (cônjuge intimation gap and stale valuation alert) and failed to quantify ITBI, leaving the investment committee with an underestimated cost picture. The skill-equipped agent scored 7/7 on judge items versus 4/7 for the baseline agent.

utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either wi_skills or wo_skills runs. Both agents completed successfully and produced output files. The file content was truncated in the display but both files exist with substantial content (11719 bytes and 9117 bytes respectively).
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is clearly more effective than wo_skills. The critical differentiator is J3 (purga da mora timing assessment): wi_skills explicitly evaluates the 15-day window under Art. 26 §1º Lei 9.514/97 with reference to the notification date and declares it expired, while wo_skills completely omits this extrajudicial-specific risk. Additionally, wi_skills produces a more rigorous and framework-accurate analysis overall: it provides a more detailed cost stack for the Barra da Tijuca property (explicitly computing the net effective discount after all add-ons), more precisely quantifies laudemio at 5% with clear SPU/aforamento identification, and more systematically analyzes the CEF financing structure. The wo_skills report, while surprisingly competent on several items (missing matrícula, occupancy distinction, comparative ranking), lacks the extrajudicial-specific procedural analysis that the skill enables, which is the core value-add for a distressed acquisition fund evaluating an alienação fiduciária leilão.

utility_details.judge_error: Both output files were truncated in the display due to size, so the full content of parcelamento analysis sections could not be confirmed for either run. The wi_skills run used the leiloeiro-edital skill via a subagent call; the wo_skills run performed direct file analysis without skill invocation.
utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced substantive memoranda that passed all verifier checks and caught the major procedural issues (Art. 887 publication violation, Art. 889 CEF intimation pending, garagem exclusion, área não averbada, embargos). However, wi_skills has a slight quality advantage: it more explicitly treated the embargos de terceiro as an ongoing title risk beyond the 'sem efeito suspensivo' label, and its structured skill-based output provided a more systematic checklist-driven risk inventory with a clear 'risco alto' rating. The wo_skills agent also performed well — better than the scenario description predicted for a baseline agent — catching all major items except for an explicit treatment of the embargos 'sem efeito suspensivo' nuance. Neither agent clearly demonstrated the parcelamento IGP-M+1%/month cost computation (J4) due to file truncation. Overall, wi_skills is marginally more effective due to the structured skill framework producing more explicit risk categorization and the embargos analysis, but the quality gap is narrower than anticipated.
